title: SpreadLine: Visualizing Egocentric Dynamic Influence
authors: Y. -H. Kuo, and D. Liu, and K. -L. Ma
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription:
Egocentric storyline of a ego network, ego is straight line. Distinction between 1-alters and 2-alters. Stronger ties are closer to the ego.  Those receiving to ego are below, those sending above. So the ordering is nearly determined by the Data. The layout generation adapts storyflow: The 2-alters that still can change their order are sorted by barycenter. Wiggle count is still minimized with lcs, but ego needs to stay straight.
The compaction step is different from storyflow: A greedy algorithm is used to compact and make lines more straight, the description is not reproducible.

title: Visualizing NBA information via storylines
authors: Jie Lin, Chuan-Kai Yang, Chiun-How Kao
layoutDescription: The algorithm is not super clear to me. First, events are spaced out so that crowded regions are avoided. Then, the ordering is computed with TSP sorting or Ellipse sorting.

title: Stratiline: A visualization system based on stratified storyline
authors: Mingdong Zhang, Li Chen, Junhai Yong
layoutDescription: Focus on locations. First, actors, interactions, and locations, get a significance score (weight). The single location order is computed with MDS based on number of characters going between locations. Actors have a global order, and this order is used to sort actors in events. Then a barycenter-like post-processing is used. Y-coordinates do not need to be computed, as locations are rows and actors are equally spaced.

title: 3DStoryline: immersive visual storytelling
authors: Haonan YaoLixiang ZhaoBoyuan ChenKaiwen LiHai-Ning LiangLingyun Yu
layoutDescription: 3D layout. The new dimension is used to encode space. So no real layout computation besides re-scaling the time axis to avoid overlaps.

title: StoryExplorer: A Visualization Framework for Storyline Generation of Textual Narratives
authors: Li Ye; Lei Wang; Shaolun Ruan; Heyu Wang; Yuwei Meng; Yigang Wang
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription: This is a User-AI collaboration tool to generate storylines from text. The StoryFlow layout algorithm is used.

title:
F2Stories: A Modular Framework for Multi-Objective
Optimization of Storylines with a Focus on Fairness
authors: Tommaso Piselli, Giuseppe Liotta, Fabrizio Montecchiani, Martin Nöllenburg, Sara Di Bartolomeo
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
layoutDescription: MILP for computing the complete layout. Characters are placed at discrete coordinates with equal spacing. Standard ILP for crossing minimization. Wiggle and Skewness (number of characters to remove to be planar) are also optimized. All these criteria have adjustable parameters with which they can be weighted in the objective. Further, also fairness of wiggle, skewness, and crossings can be optimized, weighted with further parameters. A post-processing wiggle minimization is also proposed when the ILP does not optimize wiggle. In that case, characters do not have equal spacing. Iteratively and greedily groups of characters are moved up or down as long as wiggle is reduced

title: Optimizing Wiggle in Storylines
authors: Alexander Dobler, Tim Hegemann, Martin Nöllenburg, Alexander Wolff
layoutDescription: Theory+application paper for wiggle minimization. Polynomial (Q)LP for wiggle height and quadratic wiggle height. Wiggle count minimization is shown to be NP-hard, DP for two time steps. Also, drawing style with circular arcs: width between two time steps is minimized.

title: E2Storyline: Visualizing the Relationship with Triplet Entities and Event Discovery
authors: Wang, Yunchao, Sun, Guodao, Zhu, Zihao, Li, Tong, Chen, Ling, Liang, Ronghua
layoutDescription: Multi-objective optimization ILP, where characters have discrete coordinates. Crossing minimization is main objective. Further, the objective can also contain sum of squared distance between pairs that are in an entity-entity relationship at that time. That is, events consist of multiple entitiy-entity relationships which are explicitly visualized in a matrix. In their evaluation, they only minimize crossings though.

title: How Does Automation Shape the Process of Narrative Visualization: A Survey of Tools
authors: Q. Chen, and S. Cao, and J. Wang, and N. Cao
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
layoutDescription: Survey of Automatic Narrative Visualization. Storylines are also mentioned.

title: A survey of approaches for event sequence analysis and visualization
authors: Anton Yeshchenko, Jan Mendling
layoutDescription: Survey of event sequence visualizations. Storylines are briefly mentioned.

title: Revisiting ILP Models for Exact Crossing Minimization in Storyline Drawings
authors: Alexander Dobler, Michael Jünger, Paul J. Jünger, Julian Meffert, Petra mutzel, Martin Nöllenburg
layoutDescription: Improved ILP variants for crossing minimization. Some insights about optimal solutions that lead to now ILP models.

title: Storylines with a Protagonist
authors: Tim Hegemann and Alexander Wolff
layoutDescription: Storylines with a character being in all meetings (protagonist). One-sided and two-sided storylines are considered: All characters are on one side of the protagonist or on both sides (but they must stay on that side). One-sided crossing minimization is shown to be poly, two-sided NP-hard. One- and two-sided block crossing minimization is shown to be NP-hard.

title: Visualizing Large-Scale Spatial Time Series with GeoChron
authors: Zikun Deng; Shifu Chen; Tobias Schreck; Dazhen Deng; Tan Tang; Mingliang Xu
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription: The storyflow method is used for crossing and wiggle count minimization. But then, a new heuristic is used for computing coordinates by greedy sweeping from left to right and back. This is part of a large visualization system.

title: Geo-Storylines: Integrating Maps into Storyline Visualizations
authors: G. Hulstein, and V. Peña-Araya, and A. Bezerianos
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
layoutDescription: Design study on how to integrate spacial information into storyline visualizations. No layout algorithms.

title: Design of a Process and a Container-Based Cloud Architecture for the Automatic Generation of Storyline Visualizations
authors: Emilio Di GiacomoBeniamino Di MartinoWalter DidimoAntonio EspositoGiuseppe LiottaFabrizio Montecchiani
layoutDescription: This is no implementation but a design suggestion of a system that transforms text of e.g. a movie into a storyline. Classifications of labeling etc. does not make sense, because it has not been implemented.

title: NewsLines: Narrative Visualization of News Stories
authors: Mariana Costa, Sérgio Nunes
layoutDescription: The layout is done by the Narrative chart d3 engine (https://github.com/abcnews/d3-layout-narrative). It is not clear if this really optimizes something. Probably it uses some d3 tools which might optimize crossings.

title: Crossing Minimization in Time Interval Storylines
authors: Alexander Dobler, Martin Nöllenburg, Daniel Stojanovic, Anaïs Villedieu, Jules Wulms
layoutDescription:
Time intervals group multiple meetings from a year to-
gether, they can be ordered arbitrarily to minimize crossings.
Exact ILP approaches and a pipeline: Coloring+TSP+ILP
from Gronemann.

title: NPIPVis: A visualization system involving NBA visual analysis and integrated learning model prediction
authors: Zhuo Shi, Mingrui Li, Meng Wang, Jing Shen, Wei Chen, Xiaonan Luo
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription: Sports data visualization based on storylines. The tool uses the istoryline library directly. The evaluation in the paper does not evaluate the visualization, but some sports prediction model.

title: PlotThread: Creating Expressive Storyline Visualizations using Reinforcement Learning
authors: T. Tang, and R. Li, and X. Wu, and S. Liu, and J. Knittel, and S. Koch, and T. Ertl, and L. Yu, and P. Ren, and Y. Wu
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription:
This is an extension to istoryline, where reinforcement learn-
ing is used to compute storylines which adhere to constraints
that are obtained from interactions.
The initial layout is computed the same as istoryline. Then the user modifies the layout, and the AI agent can propose different variants of new layouts, and the user can choose one of them.
Also, a new set of interactions ins introduced.

The AI agent tries to predict what a user would do to change the layout from the initial layout and the user-adapted layout.

title: HyperStorylines: Interactively untangling dynamic hypergraphs
authors: Vanessa Peña-Araya, Tong Xue, Emmanuel Pietriga, Laurent Amsaleg, Anastasia Bezerianos
layoutDescription:
The visualization has different views on data. They have data that also contains locations, and the visualization can for example use the x-axis for locations and interactions for people being at the location at a specific time.

The only mention of layout computation is that they use d3.

title: Evaluation on visualization methods of dynamic collaborative relationships for project management
authors: Qiang LuJing HuangQingyu ZhangXiaohui YuanJie Li
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription: Study on comparing different project visualizations. The storyflow algorithm is used.

title: HyperStorylines: Interactively untangling dynamic hypergraphs
authors: Vanessa Peña-Araya, Tong Xue, and Anastasia Bezerianos
layoutDescription: See other version of this paper

title: A Survey on Visualization Techniques to Narrate Interpersonal Interactions between Sportsmen
authors: Z. Abidin, and D. H. Widyantoro, and S. Akbar
venue: 2020 International Conference on Smart Technology and Applications (ICoSTA)
layoutDescription: Survey paper on visualizations of “Interpersonal Interactions between Sportsmen”. Storylines are mentioned.

title: Storyline Visualizations with Ubiquitous Actors
authors: Emilio Di Giacomo , Walter Didimo , Giuseppe Liotta , Fabrizio Montecchiani , and Alessandra Tappini
layoutDescription:
Entities are not a single path but a tree because they can
participate in multiple groups at a single point in time. The
trees are computed heuristically. Then the SAT approach of van Dijk et al. for
minimizing block crossings is applied. Then local search
is applied to further reduce crossings. Although wiggle and whitespace is also minimized, it is not described how.

title: iStoryline: Effective Convergence to Hand-drawn Storylines
authors: T. Tang, and S. Rubab, and J. Lai, and W. Cui, and L. Yu, and Y. Wu
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription:
Interactive tool to assist storyline drawing. So interactions impose constraints on the layout process. In general, the storyflow algorithm is used. 

When constraints on character order are imposed, a heuristic from [Forster, 2005 is used] which essentially is an adaptation of the barycenter heuristic. 

Constraints can also say that character coordinates must behave in a specific way, which is encoded in the alignment (wiggle count, wiggle height, whitespace reduction) step.

Further interactions also change the alignment step.

title: A system for generating storyline visualizations using hierarchical task network planning
authors: Kalpesh Padia, Kaveen Herath Bandara, Christopher G. Healey
layoutDescription: Same as Yarn: Generating Storyline Visualizations Using HTN Planning

title: Yarn: Generating Storyline Visualizations Using HTN Planning
authors: Padia, Kalpesh, Bandara, Kaveen Herath, Healey, Christopher G.
venue: Proceedings of the 44th Graphics Interface Conference
layoutDescription: Barycenter heuristic for crossing minimization.

title: MeetingVis: Visual Narratives to Assist in Recalling Meeting Context and Content
authors: Y. Shi, and C. Bryan, and S. Bhamidipati, and Y. Zhao, and Y. Zhang, and K. -L. Ma
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
layoutDescription: No layout algorithm mentioned

title: LitStoryTeller+: an interactive system for multi-level scientific paper visual storytelling with a supportive text mining toolbox
authors: Qing PingChaomei Chen
layoutDescription: Two different storyline visualizations. The first shows how topics evolve and interact over multiple research papers: The layout depends on topic frequencies in papers. The second visualizations shows interactions of keywords throughout the paper. No mention of how the layout is computed. The evaluation is weird. They show how how different questions can be answered by the visualization system.

title: The “y” of it Matters, Even for Storyline Visualization
authors: D. Arendt, and M. Pirrung
venue: 2017 IEEE Conference on Visual Analytics Science and Technology (VAST)
methodType: Heuristic - maximum independent set wiggle count
layoutDescription:
The paper compares non-standard storyline (which does not fall within our definition) with standard storylines. The standard storyline algorithm: Crossings are mini-
mized by sweep + Median heuristic. Then a local search
swaps neighboring nodes. Wiggle count is minimized by
modelling as independent set (cannot use lcs because mul-
tiple in/out-edges) and heuristic for independent set is used.
Whitespace is reduced with linear programming.
For the case where entities are grouped into locations per
timesteps and locations have a fixed order: Single node per lo-
cation with edge-weighted directed graph. Greedy algorithm
is used.

title: Storytelling by the StoryCake visualization
authors: Lu QiangChai BingjieZhang Haibo
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription: Same as the other storycake paper

title: Computing Storyline Visualizations with Few Block Crossings
authors:
Thomas C. van Dijk, Fabian Lipp(B) , Peter Markfelder,
and Alexander Wolff
layoutDescription: Exact MaxSAT formulation for block crossingsy

title: Minimizing Wiggles in Storyline Visualizations
authors: Theresa Froschl and Martin Nollenburg
layoutDescription:
Character curve coordinates must adhere to matrix en-
tries. Exact minimization of crossings, wiggle count, wiggle
height. Multi-objective formulation with ILP and SAT

title: Block Crossings in Storyline Visualizations
authors: Thomas C. van Dijk, Martin Fink, Norbert Fischer, Fabian Lipp, Peter Markfelder, Alexander Ravsky, Subhash Suri, and Alexander Wolff
venue: Journal of Graph Algorithms and Applications
source: Journal of Graph Algorithms and Applications
layoutDescription:
Minimization of block crossings, which are cross-
ings between two bundles of edges. They show poly for 0
block crossings, FPT in number of characters. Greedy al-
gorithm for meetings of size 2 which yields optimal solution
for 3 entities. Constant factor approximation for constant-size
meetings, where meetings cannot be repeated. Further, they also show NP-hardness of block crossing minimization. They also improve the FPT-algorithm for pair crossings parameterized by the number of characters.

title: An application of optimization method for storyline based on cluster analysis
authors: Yuhua Liu, Hanfei Lin, Yitao Liang, Changbo Wang
venue: VINCI '17: Proceedings of the 10th International Symposium on Visual Information Communication and Interaction
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription: First characters are partitioned into cluster based on 1D multidimensional scaling. The characters then by default (if they are in no interaction), are within their cluster. Interactions are then assigned to the clusters based on how many characters of the interaction are in the cluster. Then the storyflow algorithm is used: barycenter-like crossing minimization. Longest common subsequence wiggle count minimization.

title: Story of a 'Storyline Visualization' in High School Readings
authors: K. Osmakčić; K. Kocijan
venue: 2017 40th International Convention on Information and Communication Technology, Electronics and Microelectronics (MIPRO)
layoutDescription: The paper uses storylines for conveying a story to children in school. A user study is carried out to see if the storyline helps understand the story.

title: StoryCake: A Hierarchical Plot Visualization Method for Storytelling in Polar Coordinates
authors: L. Qiang, and C. Bingjie
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription:
Uses storyflow:Pipeline approach. First crossings are minimzed with
sweep + barycenter heuristic. Then wiggle count is minimizes
with longest common subsequence. How wiggle height and
whitespace is minimized is impossible to parse.

title: Crossing Minimization in Storyline Visualization
authors: Martin Gronemann, Michael Junger, Frauke Liers, and Francesco Mambelli
layoutDescription:
Exact algorithm for crossings minimization using ILP building on
a reduciton to max-cut.

title: Visualizing Variation in Classical Text with Force Directed Storylines
authors: Shejuti Silvia, Ronak Etemadpour, June Abbas, Sam Huskey and Chris Weaver
venue: Workshop on visualization for the digital humanities 2016
layoutDescription: Layout algorithm based on Fruchtermann-Reingold force-directed. Different forces pull interactions together, force lines to be straight, and separate different interactions. It seems that the exact y-coordinates are computed afterwards, but the description for that is missing.

title: An Efficient Framework for Generating Storyline Visualizations from Streaming Data
authors: Y. Tanahashi, and C. -H. Hsueh, and K. -L. Ma
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription:
Similar as in [Tanahashi, 2012] Pipeline approach. slots are used to as-
sign interaction sessions, as in [Tanahashi, 2012]. First, a greedy heuristic is
used to assign these slots such that there are few wiggles and
crossings. Then, crossings are minimized by greedily assign-
ing order from last timestep to the next for shared characters.
Non-shared are appended at the end. Wiggle count is min-
imized with longest common subsequence. Wiggle height is
minimized with quadratic programming, adapting [Storyflow, 2013]. At the
end a local search is used, to move location sessions between
slots. The authors claim that the layout process is for streaming data (where new timesteps may be added at the end). I am not sure though if the layout algorithm is majorly different from one that is not dealing with streaming data.

title: Visual analysis of bi-directional movement behavior
authors: Y. Zheng, and W. Wu, and H. Qu, and C. Ma, and L. M. Ni
venue: 2015 IEEE International Conference on Big Data (Big Data)
layoutDescription: No layout description, but heuristic is mentioned. Two different storyline views are presented, but both are pretty far from what a real storyline is used for.

title: On Minimizing Crossings in Storyline Visualizations
authors: Irina Kostitsyna, Martin Nollenburg, Valentin Polishchuk, Andre Schulz, and Darren Strash
layoutDescription:
Minimizing crossings: It is shown that if meetings only
consist of two entities and the meetings form a tree, a solution
with O(n log n) crossings can be computed, which is also a tight lower bound. Further, an FPT-
algorithm in the number of characters is given.

Also NP-completeness by a simple reduction from bipartite crossing number is hinted.

title: SVEN: An Alternative Storyline Framework for Dynamic Graph Visualization
authors: Dustin L. Arendt
methodType: Heuristic - maximum independent set wiggle count
layoutDescription: Pipeline approach. Crossings are minimimized by modelling the storyline as a directed acyclic graph where each event corresponds to a node. Then graphvis is used to minimize crossings (probably sugiyama-style). Wiggle count is minimized heuristically by modelling the problem as maximum independent set. Linear programming is used for minimizing whitespace.

title: Storyline Visualization with Force Directed Layout
authors: Shejuti Silvia, June Abbas, Sam Huskey, Chris Weaver
venue: The Digital Latin Library Project, The University of Oklahoma
layoutDescription:
Using force-directed algorithm only for y-coordinate.
Nodes in an interaction attract each other. Nodes connected
by an edge attract each other (same entity over consecitive
timesteps). Nodes in an interaction repulse each other.

title: Exploring Dinofun park happenings
authors: Pascal Held; Christian Braune; Rudolf Kruse
venue: 2015 IEEE Conference on Visual Analytics Science and Technology (VAST)
layoutDescription:
Real-world time series with distance values. The
quadratic difference to the real world distance plus squared
wiggle height is minimized. A proper objective function is
given, but the method to minimize it is not described.

title: An effective demonstration for group collaboration based on storyline visualization technology
authors: Qiang Lu; Xiang-yuan Zhu; Li Liu; Shu-bo Cao
venue: Proceedings of the 2014 IEEE 18th International Conference on Computer Supported Cooperative Work in Design (CSCWD)
layoutDescription:
Crossings are minimized by a sugiyama style sweep.
A greedy algorithm is used for one step, but the given refer-
ence for that algorithm is not in the paper. It is not properly
described how wiggle and whitespace is minimize, at least not understandable to me. 

Wiggle height and whitespace is minimized with linear programming as far as I understand, even though the paper says wiggle count is minimized.

title: StoryGraphs: Visualizing Character Interactions as a Timeline
authors: Makarand Tapaswi, Martin Bauml, Rainer Stiefelhagen
venue: Proceedings of the IEEE Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR) 2014
layoutDescription:
A ccombined objective function of proximity, squared wiggle height, and
crossings is given. It computes coordinates. The crossing ob-
jective is abstracted into a heuristic one (pseudo-Huber loss)
that is differentiable. Then matlab fmincon is used to compute
a solution with small combined objective function.

Proximity models that characters an interactions are together. Separation models a minimum separation between characters.

Alough there is a quantitative study, this is on the scene detection from video and not on the storyline.

title: Visual exploratory tool for storyline generation
authors: Erk Ediz Akyiğit; Tuğkan Cengiz; Onur Burak Yildirim; Selim Balcisoy
venue: 2014 IEEE Conference on Visual Analytics Science and Technology (VAST)

title: A Study on Enhancing Timeline-Like Visualization with Verbal Text
authors: J. -K. Chou, and I. Liao, and K. -L. Ma, and C. -K. Yang

title: StoryFlow: Tracking the Evolution of Stories
authors: S. Liu, and Y. Wu, and E. Wei, and M. Liu, and Y. Liu
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
methodType: Heuristic - Longest Common Subsequence wiggle count minimization
layoutDescription:
Pipeline approach. First reducing crossings, then wiggle number, then wiggle distance, and whitespace. After locations are sorted greedily, crossings are optimized as in standard storylines but with the location restrictions. This is done in Sugiyama style, barycenter heuristic considering the hierarchy.
To minimize the wiggle count in the next step, the problem is heuristically reduced to the longest common subsequence problem.
Next, given the ordering constraints and constraints on some lines being straight, a quadratic program minimizes squared wiggle height and white space given two weights.

title: Design Considerations for Optimizing Storyline Visualizations
authors: Y. Tanahashi, and K. -L. Ma
venue: IEEE Transactions on Visualization and Computer Graphics
layoutDescription: Minimization of wiggle, crossings, and whitespace by genetic algorithm with postprocessing. Interactions can be multiple timesteps long. A genome assigns each interaction an integer $y$-slot. To evaluate a genome, a pipeline approach of rearranging lines inside slots and removing whitespace is applied and then a fitness function is employed that measures wiggle and crossings. To rearrange lines inside a slot, lines are categorized and greedily made as straight as possible. Minimizing whitespace greedily shifts slot segments (Connected clusters inside a slot) down. An adaptation incorporating locations is presented, where a fixed order of locations is also encoded with a genome.

title: Tracing genealogical data with TimeNets
authors: Kim, Nam Wook, Card, Stuart K., Heer, Jeffrey
venue: Proceedings of the International Conference on Advanced Visual Interfaces
layoutDescription: Layout algorithm highly depends on problem domain but is not easily understandable. Mostly given by data itself.

title: Software evolution storylines
authors: Michael Ogawa and Kwan-Liu Ma
layoutDescription: Minimization of wiggle and crossings by using a greedy algorithm that already computes y-coordinates directly. Random order for first timestep. For each interaction, the average y-coordinate in the previous layer is computed. Then the clusters are greedily placed at the desired y-coordinate. The description is not super clear and formal
